title: Health | Beehive.govt.nz
content:
## Recognising the extraordinary contribution of nurses
International Nurses Day is an opportunity to recognise the extraordinary role nurses play in caring for New Zealanders at every stage of life, Health Minister Simeon Brown says.

## Keeping Waitākere Hospital flowing this winter
A new transitional lounge opens today at Waitākere Hospital, helping patients leave hospital sooner when they are medically ready and freeing up beds for those needing urgent or emergency care.

## Cancer treatment closer to home for West Aucklanders
West Aucklanders receiving cancer treatment will soon be able to receive care closer to home, with a new community-based infusion service opening in Henderson on Monday, Health Minister Simeon Brown says.

## New engine room now powering up Auckland City Hospital
Auckland City Hospital is now being powered by its new Central Plant Complex, the ‘beating heart of the hospital’, Health Minister Simeon Brown says.

## Appointments to Health Quality and Safety Commission Board
Health Minister Simeon Brown has today announced appointments to the Board of the Health Quality and Safety Commission (HQSC).

## New purpose-built endoscopy suite for Wairarapa Hospital
A new purpose-built endoscopy suite will be built at Wairarapa Hospital, improving local access to diagnostic services and reducing the need for patients to travel out of the region.

## Senior doctors vote to accept settlement
Health Minister Simeon Brown has welcomed the ratification of a new collective agreement for members of Association of Salaried Medical Specialists (ASMS), who represent Senior Medical Officers.

## Appointments to Health Research Council Of New Zealand
Health Minister Simeon Brown has made three new appointments to the Board of the Health Research Council (HRC) of New Zealand.

## More care, closer to home for Otago Central Lakes
Planning is underway to expand locally delivered healthcare services for people living in Wānaka, Cromwell, Queenstown, and across the wider Otago Central Lakes area, Health Minister Simeon Brown says.

## Building the future GP workforce with overseas-trained doctors
Strong progress is being made to bring more overseas-trained doctors already living in New Zealand into the primary care workforce.

## New state of the art cancer treatment machine at Dunedin Hospital
Cancer patients across the lower South Island will benefit from more precise and advanced treatment with a new $4.3 million Linear Accelerator (LINAC) machine at Dunedin Hospital.

## Appointments to NZ Blood and Organ Service Board
Health Minister Simeon Brown has today announced appointments to the New Zealand Blood and Organ Service (NZBOS) Board.

## Funding boost for SunSmart cancer prevention programme
The Government and the Cancer Society have jointly agreed to invest $1 million each to expand the SunSmart programme, Health Minister Simeon Brown says.

## Junior doctors vote to accept settlement
Health Minister Simeon Brown has welcomed the ratification of a new 26-month collective agreement for members of Speciality Trainees of New Zealand (STONZ), who represent Resident Medical Officers.

## Cracking down on nitrous oxide misuse
The Government is taking further steps to tighten controls on nitrous oxide to reduce harm and curb its illegitimate use, Health Minister Simeon Brown and Police Minister Mark Mitchell say.

## Kaitaia Hospital boosts access to specialist care
People in Kaitaia and across the Far North are benefiting from improved access to specialist health services, with new and expanded facilities now in place at Kaitaia Hospital, Health Minister Simeon Brown says.

## Volunteer Awards celebrate health heroes
Health Minister Simeon Brown and Mental Health Minister Matt Doocey are recognising the recipients of this year’s Minister of Health Volunteer Awards, celebrating individuals and groups who make an outstanding c

## The 80s called, they want their views back
A new campaign called “The 80s Calling” was launched today by Associate Health Minister Matt Doocey, with the aim of eliminating discrimination towards people living with HIV.

## Upgraded Whangārei Hospital helipad to speed up emergency care
Precious minutes will be saved in emergency patient care with the opening of Whangārei Hospital’s rooftop helipad today following a major upgrade, Health Minister Simeon Brown says.

## New appointments for Health New Zealand Board
Health Minister Simeon Brown has today announced a new Chair and members for the Board of Health New Zealand, reinforcing its focus on delivering for patients.

## Expanded role for community pharmacists
From June, it will be easier and more affordable for parents to get treatment for their children for a range of common health conditions, with expanded services proposed to be delivered through community pharmacies.

## New unit speeds up surgical care at Dunedin Hospital
Patients across Otago and Southland are already benefiting from faster assessment and earlier treatment, with Dunedin Hospital’s new Surgical Assessment Unit (SAU) now fully operational.

## Medicinal cannabis export licenses take 6.4 working days in 2026
Cutting red tape to speed up medicinal cannabis export licensing is growing the export industry in New Zealand, Associate Health Minister David Seymour says.

## Nelson Hospital ED expansion complete
A major expansion of Nelson Hospital’s emergency department has been completed, boosting capacity and strengthening care for patients across the region, Health Minister Simeon Brown says.
